hero.name: Lais Melo
hero.description: Specialist in building modern, fast websites focused on user experience.

about.label: About me
about.title:
Frontend
Developer
about.paragraph1: I am a Software Developer specialising in Frontend development, with knowledge and experience that spans the full stack. Over the years I developed a strong affinity with Frontend — it is where I have worked the most and where I feel most comfortable, both in my final-year project Viagens em Casa and in the professional project PFIRE.
about.paragraph2: I am currently deepening my Backend knowledge, building a more complete view of web development. I work with technologies such as React.js, Next.js, Node.js, FastAPI and GraphQL, and for interface design I use Tailwind CSS and Material UI, with MySQL, MongoDB and Firebase databases.

viagens.title: Viagens em Casa
viagens.desc: E-commerce + ticketing with auth, transactional emails and admin panel. Real client case.

pfire.title: Pfire
pfire.desc: Pfire is an application for generating security inspection reports.

psafe.title: Psafe365 Cloud
psafe.desc: Web FTP manager on a Linux server — hierarchical permissions, JWT password recovery and transactional emails via Brevo.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title:
Let's work
together?
contact.description:
I am available for new projects and opportunities.
Don't hesitate to get in touch!

viagens.label: Project 01
viagens.desc1: This was my final-year university project — a digital platform I built to promote the local economy of less-populated regions, connecting small producers and traders to the online market. It includes a regional-products marketplace and a virtual ticketing system for events and cultural attractions.
viagens.desc2: I focused on making the interface accessible to users with limited tech experience, with multi-language support via i18next to also reach emigrants and international tourists. The project was built with Next.js and Tailwind CSS on the frontend, Firebase for authentication, real-time database and file storage via Cloud Storage, and SendGrid for sending tickets and email notifications. Deployment is managed through GitHub Actions.
viagens.contextDesc: The platform was developed as a demonstration project, proving the technical feasibility of a marketplace dedicated to local commerce and tourism. Although not in production, it represents a concrete solution for the digitisation of regions with lower technological development, encouraging the adoption of technology in the tourism and commercial sectors.

i18n.label: Internationalisation
i18n.desc: PT, ES and EN support via i18next

auth.label: Secure authentication
auth.desc: Email, Google OAuth and reCAPTCHA v3

reviews.label: Ratings & comments
reviews.desc: Customer feedback on products and events

mercado.title: Regional Market
mercado.desc: Typical gastronomic products from various regions of the country, with direct delivery to the consumer. Cart, authentication and management panel.

bilheteira.title: Museums & Events
bilheteira.desc: Online ticket sales for museums, exhibitions and cultural events with ticket delivery by email via SendGrid.

pfire.label: Project 02
pfire.desc1: Pfire was created to digitise and organise the security inspection process. Companies that manage emergency devices — such as fire extinguishers, exits, hydrants and hoses — need to issue periodic inspection reports per client, in a standardised and traceable way. The application solves exactly that problem.
pfire.desc2: To do so, it offers a system based on reusable templates. Each template defines the report structure with custom fields that repeat across all inspections of that type. From a single template, multiple reports can be generated, each associated with a client.
pfire.desc3: Each created template can also be cloned to create variations without starting from scratch. Technically, it is a FARM Stack with a FastAPI (Python) backend and a React frontend.

templates.title: Report Templates
templates.desc: Creation of reusable templates for inspections. Each template defines the name and custom fields that will appear in all reports generated from it.

cloning.title: Template Cloning
cloning.desc: Duplicate an existing template with one click — ideal for creating inspection variations without reconfiguring fields from scratch.

fields.title: Custom Fields
fields.desc: Each template accepts additional fields defined by the user. Values are filled per report, adapting the form to any type of inspection.

clients.title: Reports per Client
clients.desc: Each report is linked to a client (with tax ID), the base template and creation date — allowing filtering and searching by client name or other fields.

status.title: Report Status
status.desc: Reports with status control (Active / Inactive) — simplifies lifecycle management of each inspection and filtering by current status.

export.title: Data Export
export.desc: Export reports directly from the listing, with all fields — including template custom fields — ready for formal delivery.

devices.title: Device Management
devices.desc: Registration of emergency devices — extinguishers, exits, hydrants and hoses — with a unique ID number per inspected device.

socialAuth.title: Social Authentication
socialAuth.desc: Login via Google and Microsoft with Firebase Authentication — JWT tokens validated by the FastAPI backend on every protected request.

nosql.title: NoSQL Database
nosql.desc: MongoDB as the persistence layer: flexible documents that accommodate optional fields and variable structures without schema migrations.

step1.title: Create Template
step1.desc: Define the template name and add custom fields. The template can be cloned at any time to generate variations.

step2.title: Generate Report
step2.desc: Select a template, associate a client (with tax ID) and fill in the fields — including the custom fields defined in the template.

step3.title: Manage & Export
step3.desc: Browse the report history by template, filter by client or status (Active / Inactive), and export data for formal delivery.

frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.desc: React SPA with React Router for navigation between reports, dynamic forms with client-side validation and a responsive interface.

backend.label: Backend
backend.desc: Asynchronous REST API in Python with FastAPI. Schemas validated via Pydantic, routes protected by JWT (Firebase), and Motor as async driver for MongoDB.

database.label: Database
database.desc: JSON documents with a free schema to support reports with variable fields per device or inspection type, without requiring migrations.

auth.label: Authentication
auth.desc: OAuth 2.0 via Firebase with Google and Microsoft providers. The ID token is verified by the backend on every API call to ensure secure sessions.

s0.desc: Authentication via Google or Microsoft with Firebase — secure, fast access without needing to create an account.

s1.desc: Main panel with all created inspection templates. Each card shows the template name and allows editing, cloning or accessing the associated reports.

s2.desc: Form to define a new inspection template: template name and custom fields that will be filled in each report generated from it.

s3.desc: List of all reports generated for a template. Each entry shows the client (with tax ID), creation date and status — with integrated filters and search.

s4.desc: Final version of the report exported to PDF, with all fields filled in (including template custom fields), ready for formal delivery to the client.

psafe.label: Project 03
psafe.desc1: The main challenge of this project was the complete overhaul of the frontend, originally developed in plain PHP. The entire interface was rebuilt from scratch using React and Tailwind CSS, focusing on componentisation, code reuse, responsiveness, client-side navigation with React Router and dark/light theme support.
psafe.desc2: The backend was kept in plain PHP, without any framework, handling file management on a Linux server via FTP (FileZilla). Additionally, features such as JWT authentication for the password recovery flow and transactional emails via Brevo were implemented.

frontendRewrite.title: Frontend Overhaul: PHP → React
frontendRewrite.desc: The frontend originally existed in plain PHP, mixed with server logic. It was rewritten from scratch in React with Tailwind CSS — clear separation between client and server, reusable components, dark/light theme and responsive design.

linuxServer.title: Shared Linux Server
linuxServer.desc: The server is a Linux variant with pre-configured user credentials. Each profile has access defined directly in the operating system.

ftpExplorer.title: FTP File Explorer
ftpExplorer.desc: Create and delete subfolders, upload files — the entire structure reflects what physically exists on the Linux server, managed via FileZilla FTP.

clientCreation.title: Client Creation
clientCreation.desc: The Company User creates sub-users (Client type) directly in the database, in the 'clients' table, linked to their profile.

permissions.title: Permissions & Activation Date
permissions.desc: For each Client, the Company User configures: which folder they can access, the privileges (create / upload / rename / delete) and the date from which access becomes active.

jwtRecovery.title: Password Recovery via JWT
jwtRecovery.desc: The user enters their email — if registered, a JWT token is generated, stored in the database (MySQL) and sent by email via Brevo with a recovery link.

transEmail.title: Transactional Email — Brevo
transEmail.desc: Integration with Brevo for sending the password recovery email. Only users with a registered email receive the link with the JWT token.

admin.desc: User with maximum privilege on the platform. Credentials are created directly on the Linux server.

points.p0: Views all folders available on the server
points.p1: Full privileges over the entire file and subfolder structure

company.desc: User with a predefined personal folder on the Linux server. Manages their space and their Clients.

points.p0: On the server, exclusive access to their predefined Linux folder
points.p1: In the app, can create and delete subfolders and upload files
points.p2: Creates Client-type sub-users — registered in the 'clients' table in the database
points.p3: For each Client, defines: which folder they can access, with what privileges, and the activation date

client.desc: Sub-user created by the Company User, with configured and restricted access.

points.p0: Registered in the database and linked to the Company User who created them
points.p1: Access only to the folder configured by the Company User
points.p2: Individual privileges defined: create subfolders / upload files / rename / delete
points.p3: Access conditional on the activation date — only becomes active from the configured date

frontend.label: Frontend
frontend.desc: Complete interface reconstruction, originally in plain PHP. Rewritten in React with Tailwind CSS — reusable components, dark/light theme persisted in localStorage, fully responsive layout and client-side navigation with React Router. Clear frontend/backend separation via REST API.

backend.label: Backend
backend.desc: No framework — native PHP processing HTTP requests, managing sessions, communicating with the FTP server via FileZilla and executing MySQL queries.

database.label: Database
database.desc: Relational modelling (MySQL Workbench) for users, clients, shared folders, permissions and activation dates.

storage.label: Storage
storage.desc: Files reside on a Linux server. Uploading and folder structure are managed via the FTP protocol with FileZilla Server.

auth.label: Authentication
auth.desc: On password recovery, a JWT token is generated and stored in a dedicated MySQL table. The recovery link includes the token and has a defined expiry.

email.label: Transactional Email
email.desc: The password recovery email is sent via Brevo with the link containing the JWT. It is only sent if the email is registered in the database.

s0.desc: Main view of the file explorer showing the folder and file structure available to the authenticated user.

s1.desc: Folder upload interface — allows transferring complete directories to the Linux server, respecting the privileges configured for each user.

s2.desc: Confirmation state after a file has been uploaded to the server — reflects the real-time update of the folder structure.

s3.desc: Visual confirmation after a file transfer has been completed successfully on the Linux server via FTP.

performance.title: Performance
performance.desc: Frontend load time reduced by ~35% after rewrite in React and asset optimisations.

security.title: Security
security.desc: Secure password recovery with JWT; tokens stored in MySQL with expiry and rotation.

scalability.title: Scalability
scalability.desc: Frontend/backend separation facilitates independent deploys and future migration to a dedicated API.

ux.title: UX / Accessibility
ux.desc: Persistent dark/light theme, client-side navigation and responsive design — better experience on mobile devices.

operational.title: Operational
operational.desc: Direct upload workflow to Linux server via FTP preserved; logs and visual confirmations implemented.

maintainability.title: Maintainability
maintainability.desc: Modular code and reusable components reduce development time for new features.
